fields.description:
## Problem
Document the code-health surface for client adoption. GROUNDED FACTS (verified against the dependency tickets): a `[metrics]` pip extra is created by `[rebar:9597-1b85-d2ab-4eed]` containing only `lizard`; `scc` and `jscpd` are EXTERNAL PATH CLIs (not pip deps, not in the extra); the `[code_health]` config section is created by `[rebar:e7a0-acef-ec67-42bb]`.

## Approach (G6/T1)
Document the three-part adoption (`pip install rebar[metrics]` for lizard + `scc` + `jscpd` on PATH) and the analyzer/fallback contract in the existing docs, and EXTEND the existing `tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py` (created by ticket 121b) rather than adding a parallel test.

## Acceptance criteria (observable + proving command)
- [ ] `docs/reuse-surface.md` documents the analyzer seam + the `[metrics]` extra + scc/jscpd as separate PATH prereqs. PROVE (new assertion in the docs test): reuse-surface contains "[metrics]" AND "scc" AND "jscpd".
- [ ] `docs/config.md` documents the `[code_health]` keys/types/defaults. PROVE: docs/config.md contains "[code_health]".
- [ ] `docs/user-guide.md` documents the three-part install + the "no analyzer -> Unavailable" fallback. PROVE: user-guide contains "rebar[metrics]" AND a no-analyzer/Unavailable phrase.
- [ ] The above are enforced by a NEW test `tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py::test_metrics_surface_documented` asserting (1)/(2)/(3); the file's pre-existing 121b assertions still pass. PROVE: `pytest -q tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py`.

## Dependencies: 0734 (content prerequisite; relates_to edge recorded).
## Files likely touched
- docs/reuse-surface.md, docs/config.md, docs/user-guide.md
- tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py (extend with the new assertions)
## Estimated scope: S-M
